stories.title: US stocks today: US stocks rise near record highs on Iran deal hopes, oil climbs
stories.summary: U.S. stocks rose Tuesday, catching up after a holiday, as optimism over U.S.-Iran talks lifted sentiment. The S&P 500, Nasdaq and Dow hovered near record highs, while oil prices climbed amid geopolitical tensions. Asian and European markets were mixed as investors weighed conflict risks against hopes of a diplomatic breakthrough.

stories.title: UPDATE 1-Florida insurer Safepoint eyes $1.16 billion valuation in US IPO
stories.summary: The ​Tampa, Florida-based company and some of its ​backers are seeking to raise up to $283.3 million by offering ​16.7 million shares priced ⁠between $15 and $17 ‌apiece.

Brainbees Solutions Q4 Results: Firstcry parent narrows YoY loss to Rs 30 crore on 12% revenue uptick
stories.summary: Brainbees Solutions, Firstcry’s parent, narrowed its Q4FY26 net loss to Rs 30.30 crore, reporting a strong 12% revenue growth to Rs 2,163 crore. The company remained PAT and Free Cash Flow positive for FY26. Discover wellness memberships at [link removed].

stories.title: Quote of the Day: Robert Kirby on patience, courage and beating the market
stories.summary: Legendary investor Robert Kirby stresses that beating the market demands perspective, patience, and courage. These qualities are crucial for navigating market cycles and volatility.  Long-term wealth creation depends on emotional discipline and consistency.  Investors must focus on the journey, not just opportunities.

stories.title: US Stock Market Today | Dow Jones | Nasdaq Live: US stocks rise near record highs on Iran deal hopes, oil climbs
'India more diversified:' Sebi chief Tuhin Kanta Pandey comments on Taiwan's market ascent
stories.summary: SEBI chief Tuhin Kanta Pandey emphasized India remains a diversified market despite Taiwan overtaking its market value. Driven by TSMC and the AI boom, Taiwan's market is concentrated, while India's capital spans broad economic sectors.

stories.title: Fredun Pharmaceuticals board approves 2:1 bonus issue
stories.summary: Fredun Pharmaceuticals’ board has recommended a 2:1 bonus issue, granting two new equity shares for every one held. This decision aims to reward shareholders, reflecting management's confidence in the company’s sustained earnings growth, scalability, and long-term value creation strategy.

stories.title: Sebi chief Tuhin Kanta Pandey backs bond ETFs, tokenisation as debt fundraising nears Rs 9 lakh crore
stories.summary: Sebi chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey called for deeper development of India’s corporate bond market to support long-term economic growth. He highlighted rising debt fundraising, proposed bond ETFs, stronger disclosures and tokenisation pilots, while urging greater retail participation and reduced dependence on bank-led financing.

stories.title: ​Vodafone Idea among 6 midcap stocksthathit 52-week highs&ralliedup to 50% in a month
Gainers & Losers: Pine Labs, RVNL among 5 top stock movers on Tuesday
stories.summary: ​Domestic markets ended lower on Monday. LIC and Campus Activewear gained on strong triggers, while Pine Labs and RVNL declined after quarterly earnings.

stories.title: Rupee slips with Asian peers as hopes of imminent U.S.-Iran peace deal falter
stories.summary: The Indian rupee weakened against the dollar as crude oil prices surged amid fading hopes for a U.S.-Iran peace deal.  State-run banks intervened to cushion the fall.  Rising inflation concerns are prompting analysts to anticipate a Reserve Bank of India rate hike, though economists expect rates to remain steady.  The RBI is exploring measures to boost dollar inflows.

stories.title: Market Wrap: Sensex falls 479 points, Nifty below 23,950 as bank stocks falter; small, midcaps shine
stories.summary: Indian benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty closed significantly lower on Tuesday, impacted by a downturn in banking, oil and gas, and pharma stocks. Investor sentiment shifted following U.S. strikes in southern Iran, reigniting Middle East conflict concerns. Broader markets, however, showed resilience, with midcap and smallcap indices posting gains.

stories.title: Brigade Enterprises to develop 5.6 acre housing project in Hyderabad with estimated revenue of Rs 850 crore
stories.summary: Brigade Enterprises is set to develop a premium residential project in Hyderabad's Kompally area, spanning 5.6 acres. This joint venture is projected to generate an impressive Rs 850 crore in revenue. The Bengaluru-based developer, a significant player in India's real estate market, plans further land acquisitions in Hyderabad, reinforcing its commitment to growth in this key region.

stories.title: IFB Industries Q4 results: Net profit rises two fold to Rs 42.67 crore
stories.summary: IFB Industries has announced a significant profit increase for the March quarter of FY26. Net profit doubled to Rs 42.67 crore. Revenue from operations also saw a healthy rise of 12.33 percent. The company's home appliances and engineering segments performed well. For the full fiscal year FY26, net profit grew by over 20 percent.

stories.title: Union Bank's board clears plans to raise up to Rs 8,000 crore via equity and debt
stories.summary: In a BSE filing, Union Bank said the board has approved raising of debt capital through Basel III-compliant additional Tier 1 bonds and/or Tier 2 not exceeding Rs 5,000 crore.

stories.title: UBS sets $5,600 target for gold in 2026,  but says the easy money may already have been made
stories.summary: Gold's strong performance continues. UBS sees a structural shift driving gold as a core portfolio asset. This trend is expected to persist despite current market challenges. Investors are advised to be patient for the next upward move. Silver may outperform gold. The gold bull run is in its later stages but not over.

stories.title: RBI's $5 billion FX swap subscribed nearly twice over
stories.summary: The ​swap comes at a time when the central bank continues to defend ​a rapidly weakening rupee by selling dollars from forex reserves. Such a move can remove rupee liquidity from the country's ​banking system and can push up ⁠interest rates.

stories.title: ET Alpha Wealth Summit: Future-proofing portfolios for the next decade
stories.summary: India's leading investment minds will converge at the ET Alpha Wealth Summit in Mumbai on June 4 to address market uncertainty and identify future alpha sources. Seasoned investors like S Naren and Radhika Gupta will share insights on navigating evolving markets, portfolio evolution, and emerging opportunities in India's growth story.

stories.title: Hexagon Nutrition IPO opens June 5: Price band fixed at Rs 42-45; check key details here
stories.summary: Hexagon Nutrition Limited is launching its Initial Public Offering on June 5, 2026. The company plans to raise Rs 138.87 crore through an Offer for Sale. Investors can subscribe to shares priced between Rs 42 and Rs 45. The issue closes on June 9, 2026. Hexagon Nutrition is a prominent player in India's nutrition sector, with a global presence.

stories.title: No Krishna Nagar in Lahore? Pak pauses heritage renaming plan after outrage by extremists
stories.summary: Pakistan's Punjab government has reportedly backtracked on restoring pre-Partition Hindu and Sikh-era street names in Lahore. Following criticism from extremist groups and social media, the administration has deferred the decision. The Lahore Heritage Areas Revival committee had previously approved the proposal, aiming to preserve the city's historical identity.

stories.title: Europe sizzles under record May heatwave; 7 die in France
stories.summary: Western Europe is grappling with an unprecedented May heatwave, with record-breaking temperatures causing fatalities in France and prompting widespread warnings. A "heat dome" has pushed temperatures significantly above average, with France and Britain experiencing their hottest May days ever recorded. Experts link these extreme conditions to climate change, warning of a "new norm."

The decision is largely driven by his desire to let his son, Björn, grow up away from the internet and choose for himself whether he wants to appear online in the future. While the vlogs are ending, Felix and Marzia said they may still occasionally share family moments through social media.
